name: Fresnedas River Gorges and Collado Pisa Loop
name_original: Hoces del Río Fresnedas y Collado Pisa desde Huertezuelas — circular desde Viso del Marqués
description: This difficult 50.1-mile road cycling route from Viso del Marqués climbs Collado Pisa, offering stunning views of Sierra Morena.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Viso Del Marqués traverse a landscape characterized by varied terrain, including river gorges and elevated collados. The region features a mix of open plains and rolling hills, with some routes offering significant elevation gains. Cyclists can expect to encounter both agricultural areas and more rugged, natural environments, providing diverse riding experiences. Best road cycling routes around Viso Del Marqués The most popular road cycling route is Fresnedas River Gorges and Collado Pisa Loop, a 50.1 miles (80.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 42 minutes to complete. This difficult route features nearly 3,224 feet (983 meters) of elevation gain, navigating through varied terrain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Almuradiel - Viso del Marqu, a moderate 43.7 miles (70.3 km) path. This route offers a balanced ride with 1,029 feet (314 meters) of ascent, showcasing the region's open landscapes. Local road cyclists also love the From Viso del Marqués to Castillo de Mudela — circular, a 18.3 miles (29.4 km) trail leading through rural areas and towards historical points, often completed in about 1 hour 21 minutes. Road cycling around Viso Del Marqués is defined by river gorges, collados, and open plains. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy circular routes to advanced road cycling trails Viso Del Marqués with significant climbs. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 290 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Viso Del Marqués's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Viso Del Marqués?
A: There are 13 road cycling routes around Viso Del Marqués, offering a variety of experiences across the region's diverse terrain.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Viso Del Marqués?
A: Yes, beginners can enjoy routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1246865552/from-viso-del-marques-to-castillo-de-mudela-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Viso del Marqués to Castillo de Mudela — circular&lt;/a&gt;. This easy 18.3-mile (29.4 km) trail leads through rural areas and towards historical points, with a manageable elevation gain of 238 feet (73 meters).

Q: What are some challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Experienced cyclists seeking a challenge can tackle routes with significant elevation gains. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1273156311/fresnedas-river-gorges-and-collado-pisa-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fresnedas River Gorges and Collado Pisa Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 50.1-mile (80.7 km) route featuring nearly 3,224 feet (983 meters) of ascent. Another demanding option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1092434874/castellar-de-santiago-from-almuradiel" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castellar de Santiago from Almuradiel&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 82.1 km with over 1,000 meters of climbing.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, several routes are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the easy &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1246865552/from-viso-del-marques-to-castillo-de-mudela-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Viso del Marqués to Castillo de Mudela — circular&lt;/a&gt; and the more challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1273156311/fresnedas-river-gorges-and-collado-pisa-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fresnedas River Gorges and Collado Pisa Loop&lt;/a&gt;. The difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1080398353/valdepenas-and-villanueva-de-los-infantes-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdepeñas and Villanueva de los Infantes Loop&lt;/a&gt; is also a circular option.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on road cycling routes around Viso Del Marqués?
A: The region offers diverse scenery, from river gorges and elevated collados to open plains and rolling hills. You'll encounter both agricultural landscapes and more rugged, natural environments, providing varied visual experiences throughout your ride.

Q: Are there any scenic viewpoints or natural attractions along the routes?
A: Yes, the area boasts several natural attractions. While cycling, you might be near highlights such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5620563" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balcón de Despeñaperros Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5418434" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Desfiladero Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, offering stunning panoramic views. You could also explore natural monuments like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5289848" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charco del Negrillo Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5337911" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salto del Fraile Waterfall and Spring&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What historical sites or landmarks can be explored near the road cycling routes?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, you can discover historical sites. The route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1246865552/from-viso-del-marques-to-castillo-de-mudela-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Viso del Marqués to Castillo de Mudela — circular&lt;/a&gt; leads towards historical points. Other nearby attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4072358" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Empedraillo&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4215375" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruins of Castillo de Castro Ferral&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4072310" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Américas Farmhouse and Moorish Oven&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for road biking in Viso Del Marqués?
A: The region's climate generally allows for road cycling through much of the year. Spring and autumn typically offer the most pleasant temperatures for longer rides, avoiding the peak heat of summer and potential colder conditions in winter. Always check local weather forecasts before heading out.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling options?
A: For families or those looking for a more relaxed ride, the easier routes are ideal. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1246865552/from-viso-del-marques-to-castillo-de-mudela-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Viso del Marqués to Castillo de Mudela — circular&lt;/a&gt; is a good choice, being shorter and less demanding, making it suitable for a family outing.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Viso Del Marqués?
A: Road cyclists frequently praise the varied terrain, which includes river gorges, collados, and open plains, offering diverse riding experiences. The network provides options for different ability levels, from easy circular routes to advanced trails with significant climbs, catering to a wide range of preferences.

Q: Are there any moderate-difficulty routes for intermediate cyclists?
A: Intermediate cyclists will find several moderate routes. A popular choice is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1085915474/almuradiel-viso-del-marqu" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almuradiel - Viso del Marqu&lt;/a&gt;, a 43.7-mile (70.3 km) path with a balanced ride and 1,029 feet (314 meters) of ascent, showcasing the region's open landscapes without being overly strenuous.
tips:
In addition to the Palace of the Marqués de Santa Cruz, which is a Renaissance jewel built between 1564 and 1586 by the Spanish sailor Álvaro de Bazán, Viso del Marqués is home to other places of interest:


Church of Our Lady of the Assumption: This Gothic-Renaissance style church has a central nave offset from the presbytery, built on the remains of a previous church. On its walls, you will find the curious “Lagarto del Viso”, a stuffed crocodile brought from the Nile by the Marquis during one of his expeditions.


Plaza del Pradillo: The center of the town is surrounded by the Marqués Palace, the Church of Our Lady of the Assumption and the Tower of the old convent.


AVAN Natural Sciences Museum: An interesting place to explore nature and biodiversity.


Hermitage of San Andrés and forest park of the Valle de los Perales: A natural environment where you can enjoy the beauty of the Sierra de Viso del Marqués


Viso del Marqués is a destination full of history and charm! 🏰🌿
Shady area of the park where you can stop before the real climbing starts and fill up your jerry cans. Incredible views of the Fresneda river gorge.
Leaving Huertezuelas behind you still have 5km to go up to 1000 masl. The climb gains an elevation of almost 300m with an average gradient of 5.6%, so take your time to admire the incredible views.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is a circular one starting from Viso del Marqués. You can typically find parking within the town itself to begin your ride.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: Given its location in Sierra Morena, spring and autumn are generally the best times to cycle this route. The weather is milder, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the colder, potentially wetter conditions of winter. This allows for a more comfortable experience on the demanding climbs.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle the "Hoces del Río Fresnedas y Collado Pisa desde Huertezuelas — circular desde Viso del Marqués" route. It traverses public roads and natural areas.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route suitable for dogs?
faqs.answer: This route is designed for racebikes and is a challenging road cycling experience. It is not suitable for dogs, as they would not be able to keep pace or safely navigate the distance and terrain alongside a road bike.

faqs.question: How challenging is the Collado Pisa climb on this route?
faqs.answer: The Collado Pisa climb is a significant challenge, known for its demanding gradients consistently between 6-8%, with some sections reaching double-digit percentages. It's described as tough and often deceptive, featuring multiple short, hard ramps followed by brief descents. The initial 2 km after crossing the Rigüelo river are particularly challenging.

faqs.question: What kind of road surface can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: The route is primarily paved, making it suitable for road bikes. While roads in the Sierra Morena are generally quiet, some sections might have varying surface quality. The road surface on the Collado Pisa ascent improves considerably and widens after passing San Lorenzo de Calatrava.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and wildlife might I encounter?
faqs.answer: You'll cycle through the picturesque Sierra Morena, a Biosphere Reserve known for its wild, isolated, and well-preserved Mediterranean forest. Expect dense vegetation, hills, and stunning natural spaces. Along the Collado Pisa climb, you'll be rewarded with views of the Garganta del Montoro. The low population density also contributes to less traffic, enhancing the natural experience.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest or views along the way?
faqs.answer: Key points of interest include the demanding ascent to Collado Pisa from Huertezuelas, offering panoramic views. You might also pass by the San Bernardo Convent and the Fuente de las Hoces del Río Fresneda.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this road cycling trip?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and length (approximately 80.7 km), you should bring plenty of water, energy snacks, a repair kit for your bike, and appropriate cycling gear. Sun protection is also advisable, especially during warmer months, as shade can be sporadic.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginner road cyclists?
faqs.answer: No, this route is classified as difficult and requires excellent physical fitness due to its significant climbs and demanding gradients, particularly on the Collado Pisa ascent. It is best suited for experienced road cyclists.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or pubs along the route?
faqs.answer: The Sierra Morena region is known for its wild and isolated nature with low population density. While there might be small villages like San Lorenzo de Calatrava along the way, it's best to carry sufficient supplies and not rely heavily on finding frequent facilities. Plan your stops accordingly.

waypoints.name: Fuente de las Hoces del Río Fresneda
